short_description.value: HyperSIGMA is a vision transformer-based foundation model for hyperspectral image (HSI) interpretation, scalable to over 1 billion parameters. It introduces a sparse sampling attention (SSA) mechanism and a spectral enhancement module, and is pre-trained on the large-scale HyperGlobal-450K dataset (about 450,000 HSIs). HyperSIGMA provides a unified solution for both high-level and low-level HSI tasks, demonstrating superior performance, scalability, robustness, cross-modal transferability, and computational efficiency.

masking_strategy.value: Random masking of spatial patches (spatial MAE) and random masking of spectral channels (spectral MAE), both with mask ratio 0.75

pretraining.value: Unsupervised pre-training using MAE on HyperGlobal-450K, with separate spatial and spectral subnetworks, followed by fusion via a spectral enhancement module.

value.value: SSA replaces full self-attention in most transformer blocks
Spectral enhancement module for spatial-spectral fusion

spatial_resolution.value: variable (depends on EO-1 and GF-5 sensors)

value.value: Randomly select continuous channels for dimensionality reduction

sampling.value: Random channel selection, consistent number of channels per HSI

cloud_cover.value: Images selected based on cloud content
